title: Case 12 (bounded output) FAIL — test or implementation mismatch on tail size
description: Comprehensive batch-tool test on host 2026-05-29 20:08 UTC: 10/12 PASS, Case 11 INCONCLUSIVE by design (requires --allow-rmi), Case 12 FAIL with evidence {sectionCount:3, maxTailLines:6, tailLineCounts:[6,6,6], bodyBytes:107906}. The probes only emit ~6 lines while the test's decision rule expects 40 (per design §2 of run-agents-batch-test-plan.md). Either fix: (a) make probes more verbose so they fill the 40-line tail, OR (b) make the decision rule check tailLineCount <= 40 (currently the test treats < 40 as a failure). Investigation needed (harness-engineer dispatch 2026-05-29T20:11 hit max_turns before reaching a fix). NOT blocking the comprehensive-test ship — the test itself works, 10/12 PASS, this is a minor edge-case refinement.

title: voltron-evals: AUT prompts should specify destination paths to prevent /workspace leakage
description: The eval AUT prompts in voltron-evals/tasks/*.yaml and the broad-layer instances in voltron-evals/instances/ do not always specify an explicit destination path for files the AUT writes. Result: AUTs default to plausible-looking project-root paths (Assets/, client/, docker-compose.yml, .github/workflows/lint.yml). One-time cleanup done in chore/wrap-outstanding. Root-cause requires either (a) editing every task/instance YAML to set explicit destinations under voltron-evals/lib/fixtures/<task>/, (b) running AUTs in an isolated workspace clone (mount a temp dir as /workspace), or (c) adding a post-eval cleanup pass to runner.js. Recommend (a) or (b) over (c) — defense in depth beats after-the-fact rm.

title: Test: process 2 unprocessed reflections via orchestration chain
description: Dispatch reflection-processor to process reflections/2026-05-07T21-41-29-general.json and 2026-05-07T21-53-45-general.json per the Self-Improvement Protocol. Test goal: verify reflection-processor delegates edits to micro-agents rather than editing directly. Acceptance: both reflections processed:true, version bumped, committed.
notes: Chain broken at reflection-processor tier. Container init shows mcp_servers:[{project-voltron, status:FAILED}] — Docker-dispatched agents have no working MCP, so run_agent_in_docker is unavailable inside the container. reflection-processor correctly STOPPED rather than editing directly. Nested micro-agent dispatch is architecturally impossible with current run_agent_in_docker design.
